svg.text: Validation qualitative cases: axis-asymmetric geometry effects
Selected from the B811 validation surface. Lower errors are better; delta is base error minus geometry error.
Geometry greatly reduces heading error, but worsens distance; motivates axis-decoupled readout.
Geometry greatly reduces heading error, but worsens distance; motivates axis-decoupled readout.
A positive control where the geometry branch improves both heading and distance.
A positive control where the geometry branch improves both heading and distance.
The two images are identical, yet the geometry branch predicts nonzero motion; motivates legal-state snapping/protection.
Base is already accurate, while the geometry branch degrades the prediction.
